highlights.text: I wrote on Friday at 4pm: “The bull case Monday is that ES can do some back-testing of the zones we broke down yesterday and today.
I wrote Tuesday at 4pm: “ My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
My lean was yes, stating yesterday at 4pm: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
I call this “elevator down” and it sees ES lose many supports rapidly.
2) All “elevator down” sells in ES resolve with a violent short squeeze.
These short squeezes are caused by my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.
A Failed Breakdown is when price sweeps a low, traps shorts, then recovers.
As a result we went elevator down into Friday’s 6240 low.
Failed Breakdown, then short squeeze to revisit 6374.
On Friday at 230PM, ES put in a massive Failed Breakdown of a low at 6245.
Then the cycle restarts, on Tuesday ES went “elevator down” down to 6315 low of day.
Step 2: The Short Squeeze, caused by a Failed Breakdown.
You get the drill: Hard sell, Failed Breakdown, Short Squeeze, Back-Test, repeat.

post_title: For 4 Days Now, SPX Has Gone Nowhere. Can It Crack The Range Tomorrow? August 8 Plan
